content: The linguistic teaching definition of "reflexion" as a conscious vocabulary learning practice of reflecting on word usage and context can be framed as a template for improving LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as language learners internalize correct usage by repeatedly reflecting on how they used a word vs. its standard meaning, LLMs can be trained to use explicit reflexion (the original linguistic sense of deliberate metacognitive check of output against learned norms) to fix inconsistent reasoning. This creates a new training paradigm that grounds abstract AI self-reflection in a structured, human learning workflow.
